senses.definition: Happening or suitable for the time immediately after the evening meal, especially a formal one.
examples: He gave an afterdinner speech at the charity banquet.
They enjoyed some afterdinner coffee in the lounge.
We went for an afterdinner walk around the neighborhood.

senses.definition: A continued electrical activity in a nerve or brain cell that happens after a stimulus or main discharge has ended.
examples: The EEG showed a brief afterdischarge following the seizure.
Researchers measured the afterdischarge in the neurons after the stimulus.
Prolonged afterdischarge can indicate abnormal brain activity.

senses.definition: To drain liquid from land or a container after an initial draining, especially to remove what is left.
examples: The fields were afterdrained to prevent waterlogging.
Engineers planned to afterdrain the reservoir to inspect the base.
They had to afterdrain the tanks before maintenance.

senses.definition: Small drops of liquid that continue to fall after the main flow has stopped.
examples: He waited for the afterdrops from the tap to stop before wiping the sink.
The afterdrops from the roof kept falling long after the rain ended.
You can hear the afterdrops hitting the metal bucket.

senses.definition: A result or feeling that appears some time after an event, action, or experience.
examples: Headaches can be an aftereffect of the accident.
The economic aftereffect of the crisis lasted for years.
He was still dealing with the aftereffect of the infection.

senses.definition: Unpleasant results or changes that continue for some time after an event, illness, or experience.
examples: He is still suffering from the aftereffects of the operation.
The aftereffects of the war were felt for decades.
Many patients report long-term aftereffects from the drug.

senses.definition: The back part or rear end of a ship, vehicle, or object.
examples: The crew moved to the afterend of the ship during the storm.
Cargo was stored in the afterend of the hold.
Repairs were needed at the afterend of the vessel.

senses.definition: To look at someone or something with continued or special attention, often because of interest or desire.
examples: He had been aftereyeing that new car for months.
She aftereyed the dress in the shop window.
Neighbors noticed him aftereyeing the property.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of aftereye: looked at someone or something with continued or special attention.
examples: He aftereyed the painting but did not buy it.
She had long aftereyed the house across the street.
The dog aftereyed the food on the table.

senses.definition: Present participle of aftereye: looking at someone or something with continued or special attention.
examples: He stood there aftereyeing the new model in the showroom.
She kept aftereyeing the expensive jewelry.
They were aftereyeing the empty building for a future office.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of aftereye: looks at someone or something with continued or special attention.
examples: He often aftereyes the latest gadgets in the store.
She aftereyes every new fashion that appears online.
The cat aftereyes the birds from the window.

senses.definition: Alternate spelling of the present participle of aftereye: looking at someone or something with continued or special attention.
examples: He stood there aftereying the prize.
She was aftereying the empty seat near the stage.
They kept aftereying the land for a possible purchase.

senses.definition: A secondary fall or collapse that happens after an initial fall.
examples: Loose rocks created an afterfall following the main landslide.
The miners feared an afterfall in the tunnel.
Rescuers waited until any afterfall had stopped.

senses.definition: Fame or reputation that comes to someone only after their death or long after their work is done.
examples: The artist enjoyed little success in life but great afterfame.
Many writers have gained afterfame for books that sold poorly at first.
His afterfame rests on a single groundbreaking study.

senses.definition: To feed again or additionally after an initial feeding, especially in farming or manufacturing.
examples: Farmers afterfeed the cattle during the cold months.
The machine was afterfed with more material to keep production going.
They decided to afterfeed the soil with extra nutrients.

senses.definition: A secondary period of fermentation that takes place after the main fermentation process.
examples: The wine is left for afterfermentation in the barrel.
Afterfermentation improves the flavor and stability of the beer.
Careful control of afterfermentation is important in cheese production.

senses.definition: To form or shape something after an initial forming process, usually to adjust or finish it.
examples: Workers afterformed the metal parts for a better fit.
The clay pieces were afterformed by hand to add detail.
They needed to afterform the panels before installation.

senses.definition: A person who becomes friendly only after events have shown it is safe or useful, especially after success.
examples: He was surrounded by afterfriends once his business took off.
True loyalty is rare; afterfriends often appear when you no longer need help.
She distrusted afterfriends who had ignored her earlier work.

senses.definition: Results or consequences that appear later as a result of earlier actions or work.
examples: The afterfruits of reform were not seen for many years.
They enjoyed the afterfruits of their hard labor in retirement.
The afterfruits of the decision were both positive and negative.

senses.definition: A more distant time in the future, considered beyond the near or immediate future.
examples: The plan looks not only to the next decade but to the afterfuture.
Philosophers sometimes speculate about the afterfuture of humanity.
Environmental policies must consider the afterfuture, not just present needs.

senses.definition: Activities, discussion, or events that happen after a game, match, or contest.
examples: The fans gathered for an aftergame party.
The coach spoke to reporters in the aftergame interview.
We went out for an aftergame meal with the team.

senses.definition: Multiple activities, events, or discussions that take place after games or matches.
examples: The stadium hosts concerts and other aftergames.
Our local club organizes aftergames for families.
Television channels compete to produce the best aftergames.

senses.definition: Gas that is released or remains after a main reaction, explosion, or process has ended.
examples: Sensors detected aftergas in the chamber.
Proper ventilation is needed to remove aftergas from the tunnel.
The aftergas posed a serious safety risk.

senses.definition: A smooth, continuing movement that follows a main glide, especially in speech sounds or physical motion.
examples: The vowel had a noticeable afterglide in his accent.
Skaters added an elegant afterglide to their jump.
Linguists described the afterglide in the diphthong.

senses.definition: A soft light in the sky that remains after the sun has gone down.
examples: We watched the afterglow of the sunset over the sea.
The mountains were still visible in the afterglow.
The afterglow lit the clouds with pink and gold.

senses.definition: A pleasant feeling that remains after an enjoyable or successful experience.
examples: She was still in the afterglow of her promotion.
The team basked in the afterglow of their victory.
He enjoyed the afterglow that followed the concert.

senses.definition: Plural of afterglow: soft lights in the sky after sunset, or pleasant feelings that remain after enjoyable events.
examples: The afterglows of several sunsets colored their memories of the trip.
They talked about the different afterglows that followed their achievements.
The movie explores the afterglows of love and loss.

senses.definition: To continue to go or proceed after something else has happened, often in a secondary or following way.
examples: The smaller boats afterwent the main ship into the harbor.
A second wave of volunteers aftergoes the first group.
Traditions often aftergo major social changes.

senses.definition: Good results or benefit that comes later, especially after trouble or suffering.
examples: Out of their hardship came unexpected aftergood.
He believed that some aftergood would arise from the crisis.
The aftergood of the reform was only clear years later.

senses.definition: Grass that grows again after a field has been cut or grazed, especially used as food for animals.
examples: Cattle were moved to the aftergrass in late summer.
The farmer relies on aftergrass to feed the herd.
Aftergrass provides valuable grazing in autumn.

senses.definition: Areas or amounts of grass that have grown again after being cut or grazed.
examples: The farm’s aftergrasses kept the sheep fed through the fall.
They walked through the aftergrasses near the river.
Aftergrasses are important for small farmers with limited land.

senses.definition: Relating to the state or life believed to exist after death.
examples: The poem reflects on aftergrave rewards and punishments.
Different religions offer different visions of the aftergrave world.
He had a deep fear of aftergrave judgment.

senses.definition: Sadness or emotional pain that continues or returns after the main shock of loss has passed.
examples: Aftergrief can last for years after a loved one dies.
She felt a wave of aftergrief on anniversaries.
Counselors help people handle aftergrief following trauma.

senses.definition: To grind material again or further after an initial grinding, usually to get a finer result.
examples: They afterground the grain to make a softer flour.
The engineer decided to aftergrind the surface for more precision.
Workers aftergrind the metal parts before polishing.

senses.definition: New growth that appears after earlier growth has been cut, damaged, or has finished.
examples: The forest showed rich aftergrowth after the fire.
Farmers use the aftergrowth as additional pasture.
We cleared the aftergrowth around the house.

senses.definition: Plural of aftergrowth: several areas or kinds of new growth that appear after earlier growth is removed or ends.
examples: Different aftergrowths appeared across the burnt hillside.
They studied the aftergrowths in various soil types.
The garden needs clearing of the wild aftergrowths.

senses.definition: The group of officers or crew who work at the back part of a ship.
examples: The afterguard gathered near the stern for instructions.
He served in the afterguard during his time in the navy.
The captain relied on the afterguard to manage the sails.

senses.definition: Plural of afterguard: groups of officers or crew working at the back part of ships.
examples: The afterguards of both vessels communicated by signal.
Old books describe the roles of the afterguards in detail.
The commander inspected the afterguards during the drill.

senses.definition: Guns or artillery placed toward the back part of a ship or vehicle.
examples: The sailors loaded the afterguns as the enemy approached.
Damage to the afterguns reduced the ship’s defense.
They moved crew to man the afterguns.

senses.definition: At a later time; afterwards.
examples: We can discuss the details afterhand.
The mistake was only discovered long afterhand.
They apologized afterhand for the confusion.

senses.definition: Harm, damage, or negative effects that appear later as a result of something that happened earlier.
examples: They failed to predict the afterharm of the policy.
The afterharm of pollution may last for generations.
Doctors warned about the possible afterharm of the drug.

senses.definition: A hatch or opening located toward the back part of a ship or boat.
examples: The crew climbed through the afterhatch to reach the deck.
Cargo was loaded through the afterhatch.
Water entered the ship through a damaged afterhatch.

senses.definition: Heat that remains or continues after the main source of heat has been removed or turned off.
examples: You can still cook on the afterheat of the stove.
The afterheat from the engine warmed the garage.
They used the afterheat of the kiln to dry tiles.

senses.definition: Instances or amounts of heat that remain after main heating processes in different places or systems.
examples: The afterheats from several furnaces kept the building warm.
Engineers measured the afterheats in each section of the plant.
They tried to reuse the afterheats to save energy.

senses.definition: Help or support that is given later, especially after an event or after initial help has been given.
examples: Survivors depended on afterhelp from charities.
The government promised afterhelp for affected families.
Without afterhelp, many small businesses would have failed.

senses.definition: To understand or realize something only later, after events have happened.
examples: He afterhended the meaning of her words only years later.
Many people afterhend the value of education when they are older.
She afterhends her mistake as the consequences unfold.

senses.definition: A cargo hold located in the back part of a ship.
examples: The afterhold was filled with grain.
Inspectors checked the afterhold for leaks.
Crew members worked in the afterhold during loading.

senses.definition: Hope that remains or returns after disappointment, failure, or loss.
examples: Afterhope kept them working toward a better life.
She found afterhope even after several rejections.
The story speaks of afterhope rising from despair.

senses.definition: Happening after the normal closing time of a business or organization.
examples: He went to an afterhours clinic for treatment.
They attended an afterhours event at the museum.
The bar has an afterhours license.

senses.definition: During the time after normal working or opening hours.
examples: You can contact technical support afterhours.
She often studies afterhours at the library.
The team continued working afterhours to meet the deadline.

senses.definition: An image that remains in your vision for a short time after you look away from the original object.
examples: He stared at the light and then saw an afterimage when he closed his eyes.
The bright screen left an afterimage on her vision.
You may notice a green afterimage after looking at a red square.

senses.definition: A strong mental picture or memory that stays with you after an event or experience.
examples: The disaster left a painful afterimage in his mind.
The film creates an afterimage that stays with viewers.
Her words formed an afterimage of hope in the audience.

senses.definition: Plural of afterimage: images that stay in your vision or mind after the original objects or events are gone.
examples: Bright afterimages appeared when he looked away from the screen.
The battle left terrible afterimages in their memories.
She described the dream’s afterimages in her journal.
